---
title: "Tutorials"
subtitle: "Model fMRI effects without hand-aligning design columns."
---

Start with the [golden path](golden-path.qmd): fit a first-level model,
state a contrast by condition name, carry that contrast into a group model,
and check the recovered numbers against a Nilearn parity receipt. The point
is practical: fewer fragile alignments between event tables, design columns,
contrast vectors, and second-level inputs.

The main workflow is:

```text
fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> contrast -> group
```

Use the task pages below when you need one part of that workflow in more
detail.

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [The golden path](golden-path.qmd) — start here
The whole workflow, end to end, on data with a known injected effect:
`fmri_dataset → fmri_lm → contrast → group`. Because the effect is
planted, you can watch it survive every stage and check the first-level
fit against Nilearn. Read this first; every other page zooms in on one
step of it.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Overview and workflow](overview-workflow.qmd)
Walk through the modeling stack: sampling frames, datasets, typed specs,
baseline terms, GLM fitting, contrasts, and group summaries.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Build a design matrix](design-matrices.qmd)
Create condition-specific HRF regressors from an event table and inspect the
resulting column metadata.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Parametric modulators](parametric-modulators.qmd)
Model conditions modulated by trial-by-trial continuous variables. Centering
semantics, the `center_modulators=` opt-out, and why fmrimod does not
orthogonalize modulators automatically (Mumford et al. 2015).
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd)
Create named contrast vectors from model columns and use factorial contrast
generators for main effects and interactions.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Simulation](simulation.qmd)
Generate clean BOLD responses, fMRI-like noise, and matrix time series for
method checks.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
Fit a small first-level model through `fmri_lm()`, then inspect beta,
t-statistic, and contrast outputs.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Datasets](datasets.qmd)
Represent in-memory and latent data as canonical `fmrimod.dataset` objects with
sampling frames and event tables.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd)
Summarize first-level estimates with native group-analysis and meta-regression
helpers.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Functional connectivity](functional-connectivity.qmd)
Use residualization and Fisher z helpers for auxiliary effect-size summaries.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Sketched and AR-aware models](sketched-ar-models.qmd)
Use low-rank GLM sketches and residual autocorrelation diagnostics for large
or temporally correlated data.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Single-trial estimates](single-trial.qmd)
Recover trial-wise betas with labels, standard errors, and method metadata.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Robust model pieces](robust-models.qmd)
See how robust weights suppress outlying time points before they dominate a
fit.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [BIDS and result IO](bids-io.qmd)
Translate BIDS Stats Model nodes into typed fmrimod artifacts and keep result
writing at the IO boundary.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Plotting and visualization](plotting-visualization.qmd)
Turn authored designs, timing grids, and HRFs into quick diagnostic figures.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Plugin development](plugin-development.qmd)
Extend internal registries while keeping user-facing modeling decisions typed.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Benchmark datasets](benchmark-datasets.qmd)
Load deterministic fixtures, build design matrices, and score estimated
condition betas against known truth.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [API reference](../reference/index.qmd)
Look up the main event, HRF, contrast, GLM, and single-trial APIs generated
from Python docstrings.
:::
:::

:::

## Finding the right page

Not sure which tutorial covers the function you need? The [module
coverage map](module-coverage.qmd) lists every public module family and
the tutorial that teaches it — a useful index once you know roughly what
you are looking for, though not the place to start learning.
